question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be between $90,000 and $92,000 on November 30?

Description: This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

evidences.title: Inside the 13F Filings of Bitcoin ETFs Q1 2025, ...
evidences.content: Image Inside the 13F Filings of Bitcoin ETFs Q1 2025, the institutional investors rebalance. # Inside the 13F Filings of Bitcoin ETFs Q1 2025, the institutional investors rebalance. The latest SEC 13-F filings mark the first quarter-over-quarter decline in professional holdings since the launch of the US spot bitcoin ETFs in January 2024. Filers, who must report their securities ownership when managing over $100 million, reported $21.2 billion in Bitcoin ETF holdings at the end of Q1 2025, down 23% from $27.4 billion in Q4 2024. Relatively, this is a sharper drop than the overall US Bitcoin ETF market, which declined by 12%, ending Q1 with $92.3 billion in total AUM. Professional investor divested US Bitcoin ETF holdings Q1 2025It’s not all doom and gloom, especially considering that bitcoin’s price fell 11% quarter-over-quarter, meaning a strong part of the AUM decline is attributable to market price rather than a reduction in position size.

evidences.title: November Preview: Will Bitcoin Break The Cycle Or Repeat It?
evidences.content: Bitcoin seasonality Context from the broader table matters. November’s average is the highest of any month on Coinglass’s grid, ahead of October’s +20.30% average, while December shows a far more mixed profile with a +4.75% average but a -3.22% median—an imbalance consistent with outlier-driven months. September, long maligned by traders, retains a negative average (-3.08%) over the full period. The 2024 row itself captures the push-and-pull of this cycle’s narrative: double-digit gains in February, March, May, October, and November, offset by meaningful drawdowns in April, June, and August, and a negative December print to close the year (-2.85%). Lessons From Prior Cycles [...] The practical takeaway—again in Daan’s words—is not categorical bullishness, but regime risk: “All in all, an eventful last 2 months of the year generally speaking. Whether it’s on the bullish or bearish side, volatility and big market pivots have been the theme into the end of the year.” The heat map supports that characterization. November’s distribution spans the widest extremes on record—from +449.35% at the top to -36.57% on the downside—with a two-thirds hit rate for green months and a median gain...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Q4 Seasonality: Will 30% November Returns Repeat in 2024? | tastylive
evidences.content: Bitcoin's Seasonality: The November Effect uploaded image Nov 5, 2024 # Bitcoin's Seasonality: The November Effect By:Michael "Dr. Data" Rechenthin ## In the 11th month of the year, Bitcoin tipiccally averages 55% returns. This time, we can add the election to the mix, too. There appears to be a degree of seasonality in Bitcoin’s performance over the years. Q4 has been the best quarter for Bitcoin price performance since 2016, with average quarterly returns of 55%. cherry picks 1 nov5.png More specifically, November has emerged as Bitcoin's standout month for price performance, delivering average returns of over 30%—making it the most profitable month for the cryptocurrency by a significant margin. cherry picks 2 nov5.png [...] cherry picks 2 nov5.png History doesn’t always repeat, but it often rhymes. Since the start of Q4 2024, Bitcoin is up around 8%. cherry picks 3 nov5.png Seasonality shows up in Bitcoin’s volatility too, with Q4 also the most volatile quarter for bitcoin going back to 2016. On average Bitcoin has realized a volatility of over 100%. It’s not surprising because we often witness a positive correlation between bitcoin price performance and volatility. cherry p...

evidences.title: Fidelity's FBTC spot bitcoin ETF hits record daily inflow of $473 million
evidences.content: #### Quick Take Fidelity’s FBTC spot bitcoin ETF hit record daily inflows of $473.4 million on Thursday. FBTC’s inflows were nearly double those of BlackRock’s normally dominant IBIT ETF but lower than IBIT’s overall daily inflow record of $788 million. Meanwhile, Grayscale’s higher fee converted GBTC fund surpassed $10 billion in total outflows on Thursday. ### We'd love your feedback. Take a 30-second survey to help improve The Block. Start Survey Advertisement Fidelity’s FBTC spot bitcoin exchange-traded fund hit record daily inflows of $473.4 million on Thursday — nearly double BlackRock’s $244.2 million haul for its usually dominant IBIT ETF. [...] FBTC’s inflows yesterday were 17% higher than its previous $404.6 million record set on Monday, eclipsing the $472.6 million total net inflows registered on Thursday for all U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs combined, according to BitMEX Research data. However, the FBTC inflow was still lower than IBIT’s overall daily inflow record of $788 million set on Tuesday. evidences.title: Seasonality, the bitcoin halving, US elections and the FED ...
evidences.content: When we analyse the average monthly returns for bitcoin, we observe a notable similarity to the S&P 500. Bitcoin’s only months with negative average returns are also August, at -0.54%, and September, at -4.16%. These negative months are followed by two exceptionally robust months: In October, bitcoin boasts an average return of 29.88%, and in November, a striking 37.51%, making November its best-performing month. The spring months, especially April, also demonstrate strong performance, with an average gain of +36.29%, as illustrated in the following chart. ##### Sell in May and go away? [...] ##### What Lies Ahead? In this article, we have explored several indicators to provide historical context for bitcoin's current situation. The year 2024 is indeed notable: In the spring, the fourth bitcoin halving occurred, U.S. elections are slated for November, and the Federal Reserve made its first interest rate cut since 2020 in September. As we approach October, we enter a historically positive seasonal phase for bitcoin. [...] Since 2010, the S&P 500 has averaged monthly returns of -0.56% in August and -1.15% in September. However, these two weak months are followed by the second- and...

The trade used a call condor structure with strikes at $100,000, $106,000, $112,000, and $118,000, reflecting expectations for Bitcoin to trade within a higher range rather than far beyond it.

evidences.title: Assets Options Volume & OI - BTC - Laevitas
evidences.content: This chart displays the historical price of an asset compared to the "max pain" price for the current monthly maturity. The max pain price is the strike price at which the largest number of options (both puts and calls) expire worthless, resulting in the least payout for options sellers. The chart shows this max pain price throughout the month and then rolls over to the new max pain price of the next monthly maturity after each expiry. [...] The max pain price is calculated by evaluating the total open interest at each strike and finding the strike price where the combined option premiums are minimized for option writers. The concept of max pain suggests that as expiration approaches, the asset's price may move toward this level because it represents a point of maximum financial loss for option buyers and minimum payout for option sellers. [...] BTC DERIBIT Open Interest By Strike This chart displays open interest by strike for various expirations. It shows the number of call and put options contracts for each strike price, giving an overview of the distribution of open interest across different price levels. The chart also allows users to see the "max pain" point for a specific...

evidences.title: Prediction: 2025 BTC Bull Market
evidences.content: The transaction volume data here likely only includes USD-denominated BTC spot transactions, excluding futures and possibly even USDT-denominated spot transactions, which is why the figures are relatively small. The figures before September 2017 were even smaller, likely because the majority of transaction volume came from RMB-denominated transactions. Image 4 BTC historical K-line and FilterM W D signal: green is long, red is short, and can be carried out more frequently. [...] FilterM, W, and D use only price as input variables, and the resulting filtered signals are displayed using the background color. FilterM displays the overall trend, FilterW limits the trading window, and FilterD indicates trading timing. Indicators with shorter timeframes provide precise entry points. FilterM and FilterW are suitable for all freely traded markets with sufficient trading volume. Indicators with daily and shorter timeframes require adjustment based on the characteristics of the underlying asset. ## Updated on January 19, 2025 (Update principle: no changes will be made to the published content.
